description:
You are a mission design engineer for a flagship mission arriving at Jupiter. Using the incoming Jupiter-centered Keplerian elements and spacecraft properties provided in ~/Desktop/joi_specifications.txt (representing the state 30 days before closest approach), model the Jupiter arrival in GMAT. The simulation must target an exact retrograde impulsive burn at Jupiter Periapsis (Perijove) to capture the spacecraft into a highly elliptical orbit with a Semi-Major Axis (SMA) of exactly 9850000.0 km. Use a Jupiter-centered coordinate system (Earth-Mean-Equator or Jupiter-Equator frame) and a point-mass propagator. Save the GMAT script to ~/Documents/missions/joi_sim.script and write the final results to ~/GMAT_output/joi_results.txt.

Required output format for ~/GMAT_output/joi_results.txt:
required_deltav_m_s: <value in meters/second>
final_sma_km: <value in km>
final_eccentricity: <value>

Login credentials: username=ga, password=password123
